title: urtica dioica var. hispidula
intro: Urtica dioica var. hispidula, also known as stinging nettle, is a plant species that belongs to the nettle family (Urticaceae) and is characterized by its stinging hairs that contain histamine and formic acid, causing skin irritation in humans. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of stinging nettle include reducing symptoms of arthritis, improving kidney function, and supporting prostate health, as well as providing relief from allergies and digestive issues. Stinging nettle can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
